1	<script setup lang="ts">
2	import { ref, computed, watch, nextTick, onUnmounted } from "vue"
3	import { storeToRefs } from "pinia"
4	import { gsap } from "gsap"
5	import { useElementStore } from "@/stores/elementStore"
6	import ElementSelector from "@/components/compare/ElementSelector.vue"
7	import CompareTable from "@/components/compare/CompareTable.vue"
8	
9	const elementStore = useElementStore()
10	const { compareElements } = storeToRefs(elementStore)
11	
12	const rootRef = ref<HTMLElement | null>(null)
13	const tableRef = ref<HTMLElement | null>(null)
14	
15	let ctx: gsap.Context | null = null
16	
17	const bothSelected = computed(
18	  () => compareElements.value[0] !== null && compareElements.value[1] !== null,
19	)
20	
21	const emptyMessage = computed(() => {
22	  if (compareElements.value[0] || compareElements.value[1]) {
23	    return "Now select a second element to compare"
24	  }
25	  return "Select two elements above to compare their properties"
26	})
27	
28	watch(bothSelected, async (val) => {
29	  if (ctx) {
30	    ctx.revert()
31	    ctx = null
32	  }
33	  if (!val) return
34	
35	  await nextTick()
36	  if (!tableRef.value || !rootRef.value) return
37	
38	  ctx = gsap.context(() => {
39	    gsap.fromTo(
40	      tableRef.value,
41	      { opacity: 0, y: 24 },
42	      { opacity: 1, y: 0, duration: 0.4, ease: "power2.out" },
43	    )
44	  })
45	})
46	
47	onUnmounted(() => {
48	  if (ctx) ctx.revert()
49	})
50	</script>
51	
52	<template>
53	  <main ref="rootRef" class="compare-view">
54	    <!-- Page header -->
55	    <header class="view-header">
56	      <h1 class="view-title">Element Comparison</h1>
57	      <p class="view-subtitle">Select two elements to compare their properties side by side</p>
58	    </header>
59	
60	    <!-- Two selectors side by side -->
61	    <div class="selectors-layout">
62	      <ElementSelector :slot-index="0" />
63	
64	      <div class="vs-divider" aria-hidden="true">
65	        <span class="vs-line" />
66	        <span class="vs-text">VS</span>
67	        <span class="vs-line" />
68	      </div>
69	
70	      <ElementSelector :slot-index="1" />
71	    </div>
72	
73	    <!-- Compare table — revealed when both elements are selected -->
74	    <div
75	      v-if="bothSelected"
76	      ref="tableRef"
77	      class="compare-table-section"
78	    >
79	      <CompareTable
80	        :element-a="compareElements[0]!"
81	        :element-b="compareElements[1]!"
82	      />
83	    </div>
84	
85	    <!-- Empty / partial selection state -->
86	    <div
87	      v-else
88	      class="empty-state"
89	      aria-live="polite"
90	    >
91	      <div class="empty-tiles" aria-hidden="true">
92	        <div class="empty-tile empty-tile--a">?</div>
93	        <span class="empty-vs">VS</span>
94	        <div class="empty-tile empty-tile--b">?</div>
95	      </div>
96	      <p class="empty-text">{{ emptyMessage }}</p>
97	    </div>
98	  </main>
99	</template>
100	
101	<style scoped>
102	.compare-view {
103	  flex: 1;
104	  display: flex;
105	  flex-direction: column;
106	  gap: 1.5rem;
107	  padding: 2rem 2.5rem 3rem;
108	  max-width: 1400px;
109	  margin: 0 auto;
110	  width: 100%;
111	}
112	
113	/* Header */
114	.view-header {
115	  display: flex;
116	  flex-direction: column;
117	  gap: 6px;
118	}
119	
120	.view-title {
121	  font-size: var(--text-2xl);
122	  font-weight: 700;
123	  color: var(--text-primary);
124	  letter-spacing: -0.025em;
125	  line-height: 1.1;
126	}
127	
128	.view-subtitle {
129	  font-size: var(--text-sm);
130	  color: var(--text-secondary);
131	  border-left: 2px solid var(--bg-border);
132	  padding-left: 0.75rem;
133	  max-width: 60ch;
134	  line-height: 1.6;
135	}
136	
137	/* Selectors layout */
138	.selectors-layout {
139	  display: grid;
140	  grid-template-columns: 1fr 40px 1fr;
141	  gap: 0;
142	  align-items: start;
143	}
144	
145	/* VS vertical divider */
146	.vs-divider {
147	  display: flex;
148	  flex-direction: column;
149	  align-items: center;
150	  gap: 8px;
151	  padding: 40px 0;
152	  height: 100%;
153	}
154	
155	.vs-line {
156	  flex: 1;
157	  width: 1px;
158	  background: var(--bg-border);
159	}
160	
161	.vs-text {
162	  font-size: 0.6875rem;
163	  font-weight: 700;
164	  letter-spacing: 0.12em;
165	  color: var(--text-muted);
166	  padding: 5px 8px;
167	  background: var(--bg-surface);
168	  border: 1px solid var(--bg-border);
169	  border-radius: 4px;
170	  white-space: nowrap;
171	}
172	
173	/* Compare table section */
174	.compare-table-section {
175	  width: 100%;
176	}
177	
178	/* Empty state */
179	.empty-state {
180	  display: flex;
181	  flex-direction: column;
182	  align-items: center;
183	  gap: 1.25rem;
184	  padding: 3rem 1.5rem;
185	  text-align: center;
186	}
187	
188	.empty-tiles {
189	  display: flex;
190	  align-items: center;
191	  gap: 1rem;
192	}
193	
194	.empty-tile {
195	  width: 56px;
196	  height: 56px;
197	  display: flex;
198	  align-items: center;
199	  justify-content: center;
200	  font-family: var(--font-mono);
201	  font-size: 1.5rem;
202	  font-weight: 900;
203	  color: var(--text-muted);
204	  border: 1px dashed var(--bg-border);
205	  border-radius: 2px;
206	  opacity: 0.4;
207	  animation: pulse-tile 2.4s ease-in-out infinite;
208	}
209	
210	.empty-tile--b {
211	  animation-delay: 0.6s;
212	}
213	
214	@keyframes pulse-tile {
215	  0%, 100% { opacity: 0.25; }
216	  50%       { opacity: 0.5; }
217	}
218	
219	.empty-vs {
220	  font-family: var(--font-mono);
221	  font-size: var(--text-2xs);
222	  font-weight: 700;
223	  letter-spacing: 0.1em;
224	  color: var(--text-muted);
225	  opacity: 0.4;
226	}
227	
228	.empty-text {
229	  font-family: var(--font-mono);
230	  font-size: var(--text-xs);
231	  color: var(--text-muted);
232	  opacity: 0.7;
233	}
234	</style>

1	<script setup lang="ts">
2	import { onMounted, ref, watch } from "vue"
3	import { useRoute, useRouter } from "vue-router"
4	import { useElementStore } from "@/stores/elementStore"
5	import MolarMass...
 
1	<script setup lang="ts">
2	import { onMounted, ref, watch } from "vue"
3	import { useRoute, useRouter } from "vue-router"
4	import { useElementStore } from "@/stores/elementStore"
5	import MolarMassCalculator from "@/components/tools/MolarMassCalculator.vue"
6	import BondTypePredictor from "@/components/tools/BondTypePredictor.vue"
7	import CompoundPredictor from "@/components/tools/CompoundPredictor.vue"
8	
9	type TabId = "molar-mass" | "bond-type" | "compound"
10	
11	const TABS: { id: TabId; label: string; description: string }[] = [
12	  {
13	    id: "molar-mass",
14	    label: "Molar mass",
15	    description: "Parse a formula and sum atomic masses",
16	  },
17	  {
18	    id: "bond-type",
19	    label: "Bond type",
20	    description: "Electronegativity difference → bond character",
21	  },
22	  {
23	    id: "compound",
24	    label: "Compound",
25	    description: "Shared compounds from element data",
26	  },
27	]
28	
29	const route = useRoute()
30	const router = useRouter()
31	const elementStore = useElementStore()
32	
33	const activeTab = ref<TabId>("molar-mass")
34	
35	function parseHash(hash: string): TabId | null {
36	  const id = hash.startsWith("#") ? hash.slice(1) : hash
37	  if (id === "molar-mass" || id === "bond-type" || id === "compound") {
38	    return id
39	  }
40	  return null
41	}
42	
43	onMounted(() => {
44	  void elementStore.loadElements()
45	  const p = parseHash(route.hash)
46	  if (p) {
47	    activeTab.value = p
48	  } else if (!route.hash) {
49	    router.replace({ path: "/tools", hash: "#molar-mass" })
50	  }
51	})
52	
53	watch(
54	  () => route.hash,
55	  (h) => {
56	    const p = parseHash(h ?? "")
57	    if (p) {
58	      activeTab.value = p
59	    }
60	  },
61	)
62	
63	watch(activeTab, (t) => {
64	  const current = parseHash(route.hash ?? "")
65	  if (current !== t) {
66	    void router.replace({ path: "/tools", hash: `#${t}` })
67	  }
68	})
69	
70	function selectTab(id: TabId) {
71	  activeTab.value = id
72	}
73	</script>
74	
75	<template>
76	  <main class="tools-view">
77	    <header class="view-header">
78	      <h1 class="view-title">Chemistry tools</h1>
79	      <p class="view-subtitle">
80	        Quick calculators and predictors using the dataset behind the periodic table.
81	      </p>
82	    </header>
83	
84	    <div class="tabs" role="tablist" aria-label="Tool selection">
85	      <button
86	        v-for="tab in TABS"
87	        :key="tab.id"
88	        type="button"
89	        role="tab"
90	        class="tab"
91	        :class="{ 'is-active': activeTab === tab.id }"
92	        :aria-selected="activeTab === tab.id"
93	        @click="selectTab(tab.id)"
94	      >
95	        <span class="tab-label">{{ tab.label }}</span>
96	        <span class="tab-desc">{{ tab.description }}</span>
97	      </button>
98	    </div>
99	
100	    <section
101	      v-if="activeTab === 'molar-mass'"
102	      role="tabpanel"
103	      aria-label="Molar mass calculator"
104	      class="panel"
105	    >
106	      <MolarMassCalculator />
107	    </section>
108	
109	    <section
110	      v-else-if="activeTab === 'bond-type'"
111	      role="tabpanel"
112	      aria-label="Bond type predictor"
113	      class="panel"
114	    >
115	      <BondTypePredictor />
116	    </section>
117	
118	    <section
119	      v-else-if="activeTab === 'compound'"
120	      role="tabpanel"
121	      aria-label="Compound predictor"
122	      class="panel"
123	    >
124	      <CompoundPredictor />
125	    </section>
126	  </main>
127	</template>
128	
129	<style scoped>
130	.tools-view {
131	  flex: 1;
132	  display: flex;
133	  flex-direction: column;
134	  gap: 1.5rem;
135	  padding: 2rem 2.5rem 3rem;
136	  max-width: 1100px;
137	  margin: 0 auto;
138	  width: 100%;
139	}
140	
141	.view-header {
142	  display: flex;
143	  flex-direction: column;
144	  gap: 6px;
145	}
146	
147	.view-title {
148	  font-size: var(--text-2xl);
149	  font-weight: 700;
150	  color: var(--text-primary);
151	  letter-spacing: -0.025em;
152	  line-height: 1.1;
153	}
154	
155	.view-subtitle {
156	  font-size: var(--text-sm);
157	  color: var(--text-secondary);
158	  border-left: 2px solid var(--bg-border);
159	  padding-left: 0.75rem;
160	  max-width: 62ch;
161	  line-height: 1.6;
162	}
163	
164	.tabs {
165	  display: grid;
166	  grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr);
167	  gap: 10px;
168	}
169	
170	@media (max-width: 820px) {
171	  .tabs {
172	    grid-template-columns: 1fr;
173	  }
174	}
175	
176	.tab {
177	  display: flex;
178	  flex-direction: column;
179	  align-items: flex-start;
180	  gap: 4px;
181	  text-align: left;
182	  padding: 10px 12px;
183	  border-radius: 8px;
184	  border: 1px solid var(--bg-border);
185	  background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--bg-surface) 88%, var(--bg-base));
186	  color: var(--text-secondary);
187	  cursor: pointer;
188	  transition:
189	    border-color 160ms ease,
190	    background 160ms ease,
191	    color 160ms ease;
192	}
193	
194	.tab:hover {
195	  border-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--accent-cyan) 35%, var(--bg-border));
196	}
197	
198	.tab.is-active {
199	  border-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--accent-cyan) 55%, var(--bg-border));
200	  background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--accent-cyan) 8%, var(--bg-surface));
201	  color: var(--text-primary);
202	}
203	
204	.tab-label {
205	  font-size: var(--text-xs);
206	  font-weight: 700;
207	  letter-spacing: 0.04em;
208	}
209	
210	.tab-desc {
211	  font-size: var(--text-2xs);
212	  opacity: 0.85;
213	  line-height: 1.35;
214	}
215	
216	.panel {
217	  min-height: 200px;
218	}
219	</style>

📄 Read /Users/deepakdewani1/Documents/Programs/vue/periodic-table-explorer/src/stores/bookmarkStore.ts

cc251f90-b68 → e8a26f0b-99b
2026-04-28 10:54:25
2532ac6f-968 → cc251f90-b68
1	import { useLocalStorage } from "@vueuse/core"
2	import { defineStore } from "pinia"
3	import { computed } from "vue"
4	
5	const STORAGE_KEY = "elementum-bookmarks"
6	
7	export const useBookmarkStor...
 
1	import { useLocalStorage } from "@vueuse/core"
2	import { defineStore } from "pinia"
3	import { computed } from "vue"
4	
5	const STORAGE_KEY = "elementum-bookmarks"
6	
7	export const useBookmarkStore = defineStore("bookmark", () => {
8	  const bookmarkedIds = useLocalStorage<number[]>(STORAGE_KEY, [])
9	
10	  const bookmarkCount = computed(() => bookmarkedIds.value.length)
11	
12	  function toggleBookmark(atomicNumber: number): void {
13	    const next = new Set(bookmarkedIds.value)
14	    if (next.has(atomicNumber)) next.delete(atomicNumber)
15	    else next.add(atomicNumber)
16	    bookmarkedIds.value = [...next].sort((a, b) => a - b)
17	  }
18	
19	  function isBookmarked(atomicNumber: number): boolean {
20	    return bookmarkedIds.value.includes(atomicNumber)
21	  }
22	
23	  return {
24	    bookmarkedIds,
25	    bookmarkCount,
26	    toggleBookmark,
27	    isBookmarked,
28	  }
29	})
30
📄 Read /Users/deepakdewani1/Documents/Programs/vue/periodic-table-explorer/src/stores/uiStore.ts
2026-04-28 10:54:25
5038e59e-162 → cc251f90-b68
2026-04-28 10:54:25
f4e9f4bd-617 → 5038e59e-162
1	import { defineStore } from "pinia"
2	import { ref } from "vue"
3	import type { TableColorMode, TrendProperty } from "@/types/element"
4	
5	/** Reads the saved theme from localStorage, defaulting to...
 
1	import { defineStore } from "pinia"
2	import { ref } from "vue"
3	import type { TableColorMode, TrendProperty } from "@/types/element"
4	
5	/** Reads the saved theme from localStorage, defaulting to dark */
6	function getStoredTheme(): boolean {
7	  try {
8	    const saved = localStorage.getItem("elementum-theme")
9	    return saved !== null ? saved === "dark" : true
10	  } catch {
11	    return true
12	  }
13	}
14	
15	/** Writes the <html data-theme> attribute so CSS tokens respond immediately */
16	function applyTheme(dark: boolean) {
17	  document.documentElement.setAttribute("data-theme", dark ? "dark" : "light")
18	  try {
19	    localStorage.setItem("elementum-theme", dark ? "dark" : "light")
20	  } catch {
21	    /* ignore */
22	  }
23	}
24	
25	export const useUiStore = defineStore("ui", () => {
26	  const activeTrendProperty = ref<TrendProperty>("atomicRadius")
27	  /** Periodic table tile fill: category colors vs property gradient */
28	  const colorMode = ref<TableColorMode>("category")
29	  const sidebarOpen = ref(false)
30	
31	  // Theme — true = dark (default), false = light
32	  const isDark = ref<boolean>(getStoredTheme())
33	  // Apply immediately on store init (avoids flash)
34	  applyTheme(isDark.value)
35	
36	  function setTrendProperty(prop: TrendProperty) {
37	    activeTrendProperty.value = prop
38	  }
39	
40	  function setColorMode(mode: TableColorMode) {
41	    colorMode.value = mode
42	  }
43	
44	  function toggleSidebar() {
45	    sidebarOpen.value = !sidebarOpen.value
46	  }
47	
48	  function toggleTheme() {
49	    isDark.value = !isDark.value
50	    applyTheme(isDark.value)
51	  }
52	
53	  return {
54	    activeTrendProperty,
55	    colorMode,
56	    sidebarOpen,
57	    isDark,
58	    setTrendProperty,
59	    setColorMode,
60	    toggleSidebar,
61	    toggleTheme,
62	  }
63	})
64

94dcd4e0-bd9 → 38d97e91-854
1	<script setup lang="ts">
2	import { computed, onMounted, ref } from "vue"
3	import { storeToRefs } from "pinia"
4	import { gsap } from "gsap"
5	import { ChevronDown } from "lucide-vue-next"
6	import...
 
1	<script setup lang="ts">
2	import { computed, onMounted, ref } from "vue"
3	import { storeToRefs } from "pinia"
4	import { gsap } from "gsap"
5	import { ChevronDown } from "lucide-vue-next"
6	import { useElementStore } from "@/stores/elementStore"
7	import { ALL_CATEGORIES, CATEGORY_LABELS, categoryColor } from "@/utils/elementUtils"
8	import type { ElementBlock, ElementCategory } from "@/types/element"
9	
10	const elementStore = useElementStore()
11	const {
12	  activeCategory,
13	  activePeriod,
14	  activeGroup,
15	  activeBlock,
16	  hasActiveFilter,
17	  highlightedElements,
18	} = storeToRefs(elementStore)
19	
20	const PERIODS = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7] as const
21	const GROUPS = Array.from({ length: 18 }, (_, i) => i + 1)
22	const BLOCKS: ElementBlock[] = ["s", "p", "d", "f"]
23	
24	// Mini tile metadata: abbreviation (2-letter element-symbol style) + short label
25	const CATEGORY_TILE: Record<ElementCategory, { abbr: string; short: string }> = {
26	  "alkali metal":           { abbr: "Ak", short: "Alkali" },
27	  "alkaline earth metal":   { abbr: "Ae", short: "Alk.E." },
28	  "transition metal":       { abbr: "Tm", short: "Trans." },
29	  "post-transition metal":  { abbr: "Pt", short: "Post-T" },
30	  metalloid:                { abbr: "Me", short: "Metall" },
31	  nonmetal:                 { abbr: "Nm", short: "Nonmet" },
32	  halogen:                  { abbr: "Hl", short: "Halogen" },
33	  "noble gas":              { abbr: "Ng", short: "Noble" },
34	  lanthanide:               { abbr: "La", short: "Lanthn" },
35	  actinide:                 { abbr: "Ac", short: "Actind" },
36	  unknown:                  { abbr: "?",  short: "Unknwn" },
37	}
38	
39	const hasAnyFilter = computed(() => hasActiveFilter.value)
40	const secondaryOpen = ref(false)
41	
42	function toggleCategory(cat: ElementCategory) {
43	  elementStore.setActiveCategory(activeCategory.value === cat ? null : cat)
44	}
45	
46	function togglePeriod(period: number) {
47	  elementStore.setActivePeriod(activePeriod.value === period ? null : period)
48	}
49	
50	function toggleGroup(group: number) {
51	  elementStore.setActiveGroup(activeGroup.value === group ? null : group)
52	}
53	
54	function toggleBlock(block: ElementBlock) {
55	  elementStore.setActiveBlock(activeBlock.value === block ? null : block)
56	}
57	
58	function toggleSecondary() {
59	  secondaryOpen.value = !secondaryOpen.value
60	}
61	
62	const hasSecondaryFilter = computed(
63	  () => activePeriod.value !== null || activeGroup.value !== null || activeBlock.value !== null
64	)
65	
66	const tilesRef = ref<HTMLElement | null>(null)
67	
68	onMounted(() => {
69	  if (window.matchMedia("(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)").matches) return
70	  if (tilesRef.value) {
71	    const tiles = tilesRef.value.querySelectorAll(".cat-tile")
72	    gsap.fromTo(
73	      tiles,
74	      { opacity: 0, y: 6 },
75	      { opacity: 1, y: 0, duration: 0.35, stagger: 0.025, ease: "power2.out" }
76	    )
77	  }
78	})
79	</script>
80	
81	<template>
82	  <div class="filter-bar" aria-label="Filter elements">
83	    <!-- Category micro-tiles row -->
84	    <div class="filter-row">
85	      <span class="filter-label">Category</span>
86	      <div class="cat-strip" ref="tilesRef" role="group" aria-label="Filter by category">
87	        <button
88	          v-for="cat in ALL_CATEGORIES"
89	          :key="cat"
90	          class="cat-tile"
91	          :class="{ 'is-active': activeCategory === cat }"
92	          :style="{ '--cat-color': categoryColor(cat) }"
93	          :aria-pressed="activeCategory === cat"
94	          :aria-label="`Filter by ${CATEGORY_LABELS[cat]}`"
95	          @click="toggleCategory(cat)"
96	        >
97	          <span class="cat-tile-short">{{ CATEGORY_TILE[cat].short }}</span>
98	          <span class="cat-tile-abbr">{{ CATEGORY_TILE[cat].abbr }}</span>
99	        </button>
100	      </div>
101	
102	      <!-- Secondary toggle + clear -->
103	      <div class="filter-row-actions">
104	        <button
105	          class="secondary-toggle"
106	          :class="{ 'is-open': secondaryOpen, 'has-active': hasSecondaryFilter }"
107	          :aria-expanded="secondaryOpen"
108	          aria-controls="secondary-filters"
109	          @click="toggleSecondary"
110	        >
111	          <span>Period · Block · Group</span>
112	          <ChevronDown :size="13" class="toggle-icon" />
113	        </button>
114	        <button
115	          v-if="hasAnyFilter"
116	          class="clear-btn"
117	          aria-label="Clear all filters"
118	          @click="elementStore.clearAllFilters()"
119	        >
120	          Clear
121	        </button>
122	      </div>
123	    </div>
124	
125	    <!-- Collapsible secondary filters -->
126	    <Transition name="secondary">
127	      <div
128	        v-if="secondaryOpen"
129	        id="secondary-filters"
130	        class="filter-row filter-row--secondary"
131	      >
132	        <!-- Period -->
133	        <div class="filter-group">
134	          <span class="filter-label">Period</span>
135	          <div class="compact-strip">
136	            <button
137	              v-for="p in PERIODS"
138	              :key="p"
139	              class="compact-chip"
140	              :class="{ 'is-active': activePeriod === p }"
141	              :aria-pressed="activePeriod === p"
142	              :aria-label="`Period ${p}`"
143	              @click="togglePeriod(p)"
144	            >{{ p }}</button>
145	          </div>
146	        </div>
147	
148	        <div class="filter-divider" aria-hidden="true" />
149	
150	        <!-- Block -->
151	        <div class="filter-group">
152	          <span class="filter-label">Block</span>
153	          <div class="compact-strip">
154	            <button
155	              v-for="b in BLOCKS"
156	              :key="b"
157	              class="compact-chip"
158	              :class="{ 'is-active': activeBlock === b }"
159	              :aria-pressed="activeBlock === b"
160	              :aria-label="`${b}-block`"
161	              @click="toggleBlock(b)"
162	            >{{ b }}</button>
163	          </div>
164	        </div>
165	
166	        <div class="filter-divider" aria-hidden="true" />
167	
168	        <!-- Group — scrollable -->
169	        <div class="filter-group filter-group--grow">
170	          <span class="filter-label">Group</span>
171	          <div class="compact-strip compact-strip--scroll">
172	            <button
173	              v-for="g in GROUPS"
174	              :key="g"
175	              class="compact-chip"
176	              :class="{ 'is-active': activeGroup === g }"
177	              :aria-pressed="activeGroup === g"
178	              :aria-label="`Group ${g}`"
179	              @click="toggleGroup(g)"
180	            >{{ g }}</button>
181	          </div>
182	        </div>
183	      </div>
184	    </Transition>
185	
186	    <!-- No-results feedback -->
187	    <div
188	      v-if="hasAnyFilter && highlightedElements.size === 0"
189	      class="results-feedback"
190	    >
191	      <span class="feedback-text">No elements match these filters.</span>
192	      <button class="feedback-clear" @click="elementStore.clearAllFilters()">
193	        Clear all
194	      </button>
195	    </div>
196	  </div>
197	</template>
198	
199	<style scoped>
200	/* ── Container ──────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
201	.filter-bar {
202	  display: flex;
203	  flex-direction: column;
204	  gap: 6px;
205	  padding: 10px 12px;
206	  background-color: var(--bg-surface);
207	  border: 1px solid var(--bg-border);
208	  border-radius: 4px;
209	}
210	
211	/* ── Rows ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
212	.filter-row {
213	  display: flex;
214	  align-items: center;
215	  gap: 10px;
216	  min-width: 0;
217	}
218	
219	.filter-row--secondary {
220	  gap: 14px;
221	  padding-top: 4px;
222	  border-top: 1px solid var(--bg-border);
223	}
224	
225	/* ── Label ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
226	.filter-label {
227	  font-size: var(--text-2xs);
228	  font-weight: 700;
229	  text-transform: uppercase;
230	  letter-spacing: 0.12em;
231	  color: var(--text-secondary);
232	  white-space: nowrap;
233	  flex-shrink: 0;
234	  min-width: 44px;
235	  font-family: var(--font-mono);
236	}
237	
238	/* ── Category micro-tiles strip ─────────────────────────────────── */
239	.cat-strip {
240	  display: flex;
241	  gap: 4px;
242	  flex-wrap: wrap;
243	  flex: 1;
244	  min-width: 0;
245	}
246	
247	/* Mini element tile: left border + symbol + short label */
248	.cat-tile {
249	  display: flex;
250	  flex-direction: column;
251	  align-items: flex-start;
252	  justify-content: space-between;
253	  width: 56px;
254	  height: 44px;
255	  padding: 4px 5px 4px 7px;
256	  background-color: var(--bg-elevated);
257	  border: 1px solid var(--bg-border);
258	  border-left: 3px solid var(--cat-color);
259	  border-radius: 2px;
260	  cursor: pointer;
261	  font-family: inherit;
262	  flex-shrink: 0;
263	  transition:
264	    background-color 150ms ease,
265	    border-color 150ms ease,
266	    box-shadow 150ms ease;
267	}
268	
269	.cat-tile:hover {
270	  background-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--cat-color) 8%, var(--bg-elevated));
271	  border-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--cat-color) 40%, var(--bg-border));
272	}
273	
274	.cat-tile.is-active {
275	  background-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--cat-color) 12%, var(--bg-surface));
276	  border-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--cat-color) 60%, var(--bg-border));
277	  box-shadow: 0 0 8px 1px color-mix(in srgb, var(--cat-color) 25%, transparent);
278	}
279	
280	.cat-tile:focus-visible {
281	  outline: 2px solid var(--accent-cyan);
282	  outline-offset: 2px;
283	}
284	
285	/* Short label — sits at top like atomic number */
286	.cat-tile-short {
287	  font-size: 0.5rem;
288	  font-weight: 600;
289	  text-transform: uppercase;
290	  letter-spacing: 0.04em;
291	  color: var(--text-secondary);
292	  line-height: 1;
293	  white-space: nowrap;
294	  overflow: hidden;
295	  text-overflow: clip;
296	  max-width: 100%;
297	}
298	
299	.cat-tile.is-active .cat-tile-short {
300	  color: var(--cat-color);
301	}
302	
303	/* Abbreviation — centered, element-symbol style */
304	.cat-tile-abbr {
305	  font-family: var(--font-mono);
306	  font-size: 0.875rem;
307	  font-weight: 700;
308	  color: var(--text-secondary);
309	  line-height: 1;
310	  align-self: center;
311	  margin-top: auto;
312	}
313	
314	.cat-tile.is-active .cat-tile-abbr {
315	  color: var(--cat-color);
316	}
317	
318	/* ── Secondary filters row actions ──────────────────────────────── */
319	.filter-row-actions {
320	  display: flex;
321	  align-items: center;
322	  gap: 6px;
323	  margin-left: auto;
324	  flex-shrink: 0;
325	}
326	
327	.secondary-toggle {
328	  display: flex;
329	  align-items: center;
330	  gap: 4px;
331	  padding: 3px 8px;
332	  background: none;
333	  border: 1px solid var(--bg-border);
334	  border-radius: 2px;
335	  cursor: pointer;
336	  font-family: var(--font-mono);
337	  font-size: var(--text-2xs);
338	  font-weight: 500;
339	  color: var(--text-secondary);
340	  white-space: nowrap;
341	  transition: border-color 150ms ease, color 150ms ease;
342	}
343	
344	.secondary-toggle:hover,
345	.secondary-toggle.is-open,
346	.secondary-toggle.has-active {
347	  border-color: var(--accent-cyan);
348	  color: var(--accent-cyan);
349	}
350	
351	.toggle-icon {
352	  transition: transform 200ms ease;
353	}
354	
355	.secondary-toggle.is-open .toggle-icon {
356	  transform: rotate(180deg);
357	}
358	
359	/* ── Clear button ───────────────────────────────────────────────── */
360	.clear-btn {
361	  padding: 3px 8px;
362	  background: none;
363	  border: 1px solid var(--bg-border);
364	  border-radius: 2px;
365	  cursor: pointer;
366	  font-family: var(--font-mono);
367	  font-size: var(--text-2xs);
368	  font-weight: 500;
369	  color: var(--text-secondary);
370	  white-space: nowrap;
371	  transition: border-color 150ms ease, color 150ms ease;
372	}
373	
374	.clear-btn:hover {
375	  border-color: var(--text-secondary);
376	  color: var(--text-primary);
377	}
378	
379	.clear-btn:focus-visible {
380	  outline: 2px solid var(--accent-cyan);
381	  outline-offset: 2px;
382	}
383	
384	/* ── Secondary filters (Period / Block / Group) ──────────────────── */
385	.filter-group {
386	  display: flex;
387	  align-items: center;
388	  gap: 8px;
389	  min-width: 0;
390	}
391	
392	.filter-group--grow {
393	  flex: 1;
394	  min-width: 0;
395	}
396	
397	.filter-divider {
398	  width: 1px;
399	  height: 20px;
400	  background-color: var(--bg-border);
401	  flex-shrink: 0;
402	}
403	
404	.compact-strip {
405	  display: flex;
406	  gap: 3px;
407	  flex-wrap: nowrap;
408	}
409	
410	.compact-strip--scroll {
411	  overflow-x: auto;
412	  scrollbar-width: none;
413	  mask-image: linear-gradient(to right, black 85%, transparent 100%);
414	  -webkit-mask-image: linear-gradient(to right, black 85%, transparent 100%);
415	}
416	
417	.compact-strip--scroll::-webkit-scrollbar {
418	  display: none;
419	}
420	
421	.compact-chip {
422	  display: flex;
423	  align-items: center;
424	  justify-content: center;
425	  min-width: 26px;
426	  height: 26px;
427	  padding: 0 4px;
428	  background-color: var(--bg-elevated);
429	  border: 1px solid var(--bg-border);
430	  border-radius: 2px;
431	  cursor: pointer;
432	  font-family: var(--font-mono);
433	  font-size: var(--text-xs);
434	  font-weight: 600;
435	  color: var(--text-secondary);
436	  white-space: nowrap;
437	  flex-shrink: 0;
438	  transition:
439	    border-color 150ms ease,
440	    color 150ms ease,
441	    background-color 150ms ease;
442	}
443	
444	.compact-chip:hover {
445	  border-color: var(--accent-cyan);
446	  color: var(--text-primary);
447	}
448	
449	.compact-chip.is-active {
450	  border-color: var(--accent-cyan);
451	  background-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--accent-cyan) 12%, var(--bg-surface));
452	  color: var(--accent-cyan);
453	}
454	
455	.compact-chip:focus-visible {
456	  outline: 2px solid var(--accent-cyan);
457	  outline-offset: 2px;
458	}
459	
460	/* ── Transition for secondary panel ─────────────────────────────── */
461	.secondary-enter-active,
462	.secondary-leave-active {
463	  transition: opacity 200ms ease, transform 200ms ease;
464	  overflow: hidden;
465	}
466	
467	.secondary-enter-from,
468	.secondary-leave-to {
469	  opacity: 0;
470	  transform: translateY(-4px);
471	}
472	
473	/* ── Results feedback ───────────────────────────────────────────── */
474	.results-feedback {
475	  padding: 8px 14px;
476	  border-top: 1px solid var(--bg-border);
477	  display: flex;
478	  align-items: center;
479	  gap: 12px;
480	}
481	
482	.feedback-text {
483	  font-size: var(--text-sm);
484	  color: var(--text-secondary);
485	  font-family: var(--font-mono);
486	  font-size: var(--text-xs);
487	}
488	
489	.feedback-clear {
490	  background: none;
491	  border: none;
492	  padding: 0;
493	  color: var(--accent-cyan);
494	  font-family: var(--font-mono);
495	  font-size: var(--text-xs);
496	  font-weight: 600;
497	  cursor: pointer;
498	  text-decoration: underline;
499	  text-underline-offset: 3px;
500	  transition: color 150ms ease;
501	}
502	
503	.feedback-clear:hover {
504	  color: var(--text-primary);
505	}
506	</style>

Feature: Periodic table display
  As a user
  I want to see all 118 elements laid out in the standard periodic table grid
  So that I can explore elements visually

Feature: Periodic table display
  As a user
  I want to see all 118 elements laid out in the standard periodic table grid
  So that I can explore elements visually

  Background:
    Given I am on the periodic table page

  Scenario: All elements are visible on page load
    Then I should see 118 element tiles arranged in the standard periodic table layout
    And each tile should show the element symbol, atomic number, and name

  Scenario: Element tiles are colour-coded by category
    When the colour mode is set to "Category"
    Then each element tile should display its category colour

  Scenario: Element tiles are colour-coded by property gradient
    When I switch the colour mode to a property such as "Atomic radius"
    Then the tiles should change to a gradient colour scale reflecting that property
    And a colour legend should appear below the table

  Scenario: Hovering an element tile shows a hover card
    When I hover over an element tile
    Then a hover card should appear with the element's key properties
    And the hover card should disappear when I move the mouse away

  Scenario: Clicking an element tile opens the detail modal
    When I click on an element tile
    Then the element detail modal should open
    And it should display the element's full name and symbol

  Scenario: Colour legend reflects the active colour mode
    When I switch between colour modes
    Then the colour legend at the bottom of the table should update to match the active mode

Feature: Search and filter elements
  As a user
  I want to search and filter the periodic table
  So that I can quickly find elements that match specific criteria

 1

Feature: Search and filter elements
  As a user
  I want to search and filter the periodic table
  So that I can quickly find elements that match specific criteria

  Background:
    Given I am on the periodic table page

  Scenario: Searching by element name highlights matching elements
    When I type "carbon" in the search bar
    Then only Carbon should be highlighted on the table
    And all other tiles should appear dimmed

  Scenario: Searching by element symbol highlights matching elements
    When I type "Au" in the search bar
    Then only Gold should be highlighted on the table

  Scenario: Searching by atomic number highlights the matching element
    When I type "79" in the search bar
    Then only Gold should be highlighted on the table

  Scenario: Clearing the search restores all elements
    Given I have searched for "helium"
    When I clear the search input
    Then all element tiles should return to their normal state

  Scenario: Filtering by category highlights matching elements
    When I click the "Alkali" category filter
    Then only alkali metal elements should be highlighted
    And a "Clear" button should appear

  Scenario: Clearing category filter restores all elements
    Given I have filtered by category "Noble"
    When I click the "Clear" button
    Then all element tiles should return to their normal state
    And the "Clear" button should disappear

  Scenario: Filtering by period highlights matching elements
    Given the secondary filters panel is open
    When I click period "2"
    Then only second-period elements should be highlighted

  Scenario: Filtering by block highlights matching elements
    Given the secondary filters panel is open
    When I click the "d" block filter
    Then only d-block (transition metal) elements should be highlighted

  Scenario: Filtering by group highlights matching elements
    Given the secondary filters panel is open
    When I click group "1"
    Then only group-1 elements should be highlighted

  Scenario: No elements match the active filter
    When I apply a filter combination that matches no elements
    Then a "No elements match these filters" message should appear
    And a "Clear all" link should be shown

Feature: Element detail modal
  As a user
  I want to open a detailed view for any element
  So that I can learn about its properties, structure, and real-world uses

Feature: Element detail modal
  As a user
  I want to open a detailed view for any element
  So that I can learn about its properties, structure, and real-world uses

  Background:
    Given I am on the periodic table page
    And I have clicked on an element tile

  Scenario: Detail modal opens with element header information
    Then the modal should display the element's name, symbol, and atomic number
    And the element's category should be indicated

  Scenario: Properties tab shows key physical and chemical data
    When I view the "Properties" tab
    Then I should see values such as atomic mass, density, melting point, and boiling point

  Scenario: 3D atom model tab renders the electron shell diagram
    When I view the "Atom model" tab
    Then a 3D or shell-based atom model should be displayed for the element

  Scenario: Electron configuration tab shows orbital fill diagram
    When I view the "Electron config" tab
    Then the electron configuration notation should be shown
    And an orbital fill visualisation should be present

  Scenario: Isotopes tab lists known isotopes
    When I view the "Isotopes" tab
    Then a list or explorer of known isotopes should be displayed

  Scenario: Spectral lines tab shows emission spectrum
    When I view the "Spectral lines" tab
    Then the element's characteristic spectral lines should be rendered

  Scenario: Abundance tab shows occurrence data
    When I view the "Abundance" tab
    Then data about the element's abundance in the Earth's crust or universe should appear

  Scenario: Real world tab shows applications
    When I view the "Real world" tab
    Then common uses and applications of the element should be listed

  Scenario: Safety tab shows hazard information
    When I view the "Safety" tab
    Then hazard or safety information for the element should be displayed

  Scenario: Closing the modal returns to the table
    When I close the detail modal
    Then the modal should disappear
    And the periodic table should be visible again

  Scenario: Tabs not applicable to an element are hidden
    Given I open the detail modal for an element with limited data
    Then only tabs that have data available for that element should be shown

  As a user
  I want to compare two elements side by side
  So that I can understand the differences in their properties

Feature: Element comparison
  As a user
  I want to compare two elements side by side
  So that I can understand the differences in their properties

  Background:
    Given I am on the Compare page

  Scenario: Empty state shown before any element is selected
    Then I should see a prompt to select two elements
    And two placeholder tiles with "?" should be visible

  Scenario: Partial selection shows a prompt for the second element
    When I select one element in the first slot
    Then I should see a message saying "Now select a second element to compare"

  Scenario: Comparison table appears when both elements are selected
    When I select "Hydrogen" in the first slot
    And I select "Helium" in the second slot
    Then a comparison table should appear showing their properties side by side

  Scenario: Comparison table shows differences clearly
    When I compare two elements
    Then the table should highlight where the values differ

  Scenario: Changing one element updates the comparison table
    Given I am comparing "Hydrogen" and "Helium"
    When I change the first element to "Lithium"
    Then the comparison table should update to show "Lithium" vs "Helium"

  Scenario: Clearing a slot returns to the partial selection state
    Given I am comparing two elements
    When I remove one of the selected elements
    Then the comparison table should disappear
    And I should see the partial selection prompt

  Scenario: Navigating directly to the Compare page works
    When I navigate to "/compare"
    Then the Compare page should load with the empty selection state

  So that I can understand periodic trends at a glance

Feature: Periodic trends visualisation
  As a user
  I want to visualise how element properties vary across the periodic table
  So that I can understand periodic trends at a glance

  Background:
    Given I am on the Trends page

  Scenario: Default trend property is shown on page load
    Then the heatmap should display the "Atomic radius" property
    And the page subtitle should describe the selected property

  Scenario: Selecting a different trend property updates the heatmap
    When I select "Electronegativity" from the property selector
    Then the heatmap colours should update to reflect electronegativity values
    And the panel header should display "Electronegativity"

  Scenario: Hovering over an element cell shows its value
    When I hover over an element cell in the heatmap
    Then a tooltip or label should display that element's value for the active property

  Scenario: Elements with no data for the selected property are visually distinct
    When I select a property where some elements have no data
    Then elements without data should appear greyed out or empty

  Scenario: Navigating directly to the Trends page works
    When I navigate to "/trends"
    Then the Trends page should load with the default property heatmap

  As a user
  I want to test my knowledge of the periodic table
  So that I can learn element symbols, names, and properties through practice

Feature: Quiz mode
  As a user
  I want to test my knowledge of the periodic table
  So that I can learn element symbols, names, and properties through practice

  Background:
    Given I am on the Quiz page

  Scenario: Quiz menu shows available quiz modes
    Then I should see a selection of quiz modes to choose from
    And I should not yet be in a quiz session

  Scenario: Starting a flashcard quiz enters the playing phase
    When I select the flashcard quiz mode
    Then I should see a flashcard showing an element
    And a scoreboard with my current score and streak should be visible

  Scenario: Answering a flashcard as correct increases the score
    Given I am in a flashcard quiz session
    When I mark the current flashcard as correct
    Then my score should increase by one
    And the next flashcard should be shown

  Scenario: Answering a flashcard as incorrect does not increase the score
    Given I am in a flashcard quiz session
    When I mark the current flashcard as incorrect
    Then my score should remain the same
    And the next flashcard should be shown

  Scenario: Skipping a flashcard moves to the next without scoring
    Given I am in a flashcard quiz session
    When I skip the current flashcard
    Then the next flashcard should be shown
    And my score should remain unchanged

  Scenario: Starting a multiple-choice quiz enters the playing phase
    When I select the multiple-choice quiz mode
    Then I should see a question with multiple answer options

  Scenario: Selecting the correct MCQ answer reveals it as correct
    Given I am in a multiple-choice quiz session
    When I select the correct answer
    Then the selected option should be highlighted as correct
    And a "Next" button should appear

  Scenario: Selecting a wrong MCQ answer reveals the correct one
    Given I am in a multiple-choice quiz session
    When I select an incorrect answer
    Then the correct answer should be highlighted
    And my incorrect choice should be indicated as wrong

  Scenario: Completing all questions shows the results screen
    Given I am in a quiz session
    When all questions have been answered
    Then I should see the results screen with my final score and max streak

  Scenario: Results screen shows a new personal best when score improves
    Given my previous best score for this quiz mode is 5
    When I complete a quiz session with a score of 8
    Then the results screen should indicate a new personal best

  Scenario: Playing again from results starts a new quiz in the same mode
    Given I am on the quiz results screen
    When I click "Play again"
    Then a new quiz session should start in the same mode

  Scenario: Changing mode from results returns to the quiz menu
    Given I am on the quiz results screen
    When I click "Change mode"
    Then I should be returned to the quiz mode selection menu

  Scenario: Exiting mid-quiz via the scoreboard returns to the menu
    Given I am in an active quiz session
    When I click the exit button on the scoreboard
    Then I should be returned to the quiz mode selection menu

  Scenario: Best scores persist after a page reload
    Given I have completed a quiz session with a score of 6
    When I reload the page and navigate back to the Quiz page
    Then my best score for that mode should still be shown as 6

Feature: Chemistry tools
  As a user
  I want to use built-in calculators and predictors
  So that I can quickly get chemistry answers without leaving the app

Feature: Chemistry tools
  As a user
  I want to use built-in calculators and predictors
  So that I can quickly get chemistry answers without leaving the app

  Background:
    Given I am on the Tools page

  Scenario: Molar mass tab is active by default
    Then the "Molar mass" tab should be selected
    And the molar mass calculator should be visible

  Scenario: Calculating molar mass for a valid formula
    Given the "Molar mass" tab is active
    When I enter a valid chemical formula such as "H2O"
    Then the calculated molar mass should be displayed
    And each element's contribution to the total mass should be shown

  Scenario: Entering an invalid formula shows an error
    Given the "Molar mass" tab is active
    When I enter an invalid formula such as "Xyz99"
    Then an error message should be displayed
    And no molar mass result should be shown

  Scenario: Switching to the Bond type predictor tab
    When I click the "Bond type" tab
    Then the bond type predictor tool should be visible
    And the URL hash should update to "#bond-type"

  Scenario: Predicting bond type between two elements
    Given the "Bond type" tab is active
    When I select two elements to compare their electronegativity
    Then the predicted bond character (ionic, polar covalent, or covalent) should be shown
    And the electronegativity difference should be displayed

  Scenario: Switching to the Compound predictor tab
    When I click the "Compound" tab
    Then the compound predictor tool should be visible
    And the URL hash should update to "#compound"

  Scenario: Finding shared compounds for a selected element
    Given the "Compound" tab is active
    When I select an element
    Then a list of known compounds containing that element should be displayed

  Scenario: Deep-linking directly to a tool tab via URL hash
    When I navigate to "/tools#bond-type"
    Then the "Bond type" tab should be active on page load
2026-04-28 10:56:01

Feature: Element bookmarks
  As a user
  I want to bookmark elements I am interested in
  So that I can quickly find and revisit them later

  Scenario: Bookmarking an element from the detail modal
    Given I have opened the detail modal for "Gold"
    When I click the bookmark button
    Then "Gold" should be added to my bookmarks
    And the bookmark button should appear active

  Scenario: Unbookmarking an element removes it from bookmarks
    Given "Gold" is already bookmarked
    And I have the detail modal for "Gold" open
    When I click the active bookmark button
    Then "Gold" should be removed from my bookmarks
    And the bookmark button should appear inactive

  Scenario: Bookmarked elements appear in the bookmarks panel
    Given I have bookmarked "Hydrogen" and "Oxygen"
    When I open the bookmarks panel
    Then both "Hydrogen" and "Oxygen" should be listed in the panel

  Scenario: Empty bookmarks panel shows a prompt
    Given I have no bookmarked elements
    When I open the bookmarks panel
    Then I should see a message indicating no elements have been bookmarked

  Scenario: Bookmarks persist after a page reload
    Given I have bookmarked "Carbon"
    When I reload the page
    Then "Carbon" should still appear in my bookmarks panel

  Scenario: Bookmark count is shown in the header
    Given I have bookmarked 3 elements
    Then the bookmarks icon or button in the header should indicate 3 bookmarks

Feature: Theme and display settings
  As a user
  I want to switch between dark and light themes
  So that I can use the app comfortably in different lighting conditions

  Scenario: App loads in dark theme by default
    Given I have no saved theme preference
    When I open the app
    Then the app should display in dark mode

  Scenario: Switching to light theme applies immediately
    Given the app is in dark mode
    When I click the theme toggle button
    Then the app should switch to light mode
    And the page background and text colours should reflect the light theme

  Scenario: Switching back to dark theme applies immediately
    Given the app is in light mode
    When I click the theme toggle button
    Then the app should switch back to dark mode

  Scenario: Selected theme persists after a page reload
    Given I have switched to light mode
    When I reload the page
    Then the app should still display in light mode

  Scenario: Navigation links are accessible from any page
    Given I am on any page of the app
    Then the header should show navigation links to Table, Compare, Trends, Quiz, and Tools
    And clicking each link should navigate to the corresponding page
